text: ROS generation in normal cells, including neurons, occurs within homeostatic control. When ROS levels exceed the antioxidant capacity of a cell, a deleterious condition known as oxidative stress occurs (Klein and Ackerman 2003). Unchecked, excessive ROS can lead to the destruction of cellular components including lipids, protein, and DNA, and ultimately cell death via apoptosis or necrosis (Kannan and Jain 2000).
